225	// ============================================================================
226	// ReservedPoolBadge — small chip used in seat pickers, manifests, and the
227	// walk-in daily list to mark seats/passengers belonging to a reserved pool.
228	// ============================================================================
229	function ReservedPoolBadge({ pool, size = 'sm' }) {
230	  if (pool === 'regular' || !pool) return null;
231	  const palette = pool === 'govHospital'
232	    ? { bg: '#E9D5FF', fg: '#5B21B6', label: 'GOV/HOSPITAL POOL', short: 'GH' }
233	    : { bg: '#FEF3C7', fg: '#92400E', label: 'SENIOR/PWD POOL', short: 'SP' };
234	  if (size === 'xs') {
235	    return (
236	      <span
237	        className="inline-block rounded font-bold leading-none px-1 text-[7px]"
238	        style={{ background: palette.bg, color: palette.fg }}
239	      >
240	        {palette.short}
241	      </span>
242	    );
243	  }
244	  const padding = size === 'sm' ? 'px-1.5 py-0.5' : 'px-2 py-1';
245	  const text = size === 'sm' ? 'text-[9px]' : 'text-[11px]';
246	  return (
247	    <span
248	      className={`inline-block rounded font-bold tracking-wide ${padding} ${text}`}
249	      style={{ background: palette.bg, color: palette.fg }}
250	    >
251	      {palette.label}
252	    </span>
253	  );
254	}
255	
256	// ============================================================================
257	// TIER 1: LANDING PAGE
258	// ============================================================================
259	function LandingScreen({ setScreen, t = T.en }) {
260	  const [tripType, setTripType] = useState('OneWay');
261	  const [direction, setDirection] = useState('outbound'); // 'outbound' = Batangas→Tilik, 'return' = Tilik→Batangas
262	  const fromLabel = direction === 'outbound' ? 'Batangas (any port)' : 'Tilik Port, Lubang';
263	  const toLabel = direction === 'outbound' ? 'Tilik Port, Lubang' : 'Batangas (any port)';
264	  return (
265	    <div>
266	      <MobileBadge strategy="Mobile First" />
267	      {/* HERO */}

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 22:49:07Z)**_

This is a purely presentational change in a documentation mockup file (`docs/vercel_mockup/`). The `ReservedPoolBadge` component:

- Receives only two props: `pool` (string, used only as an equality check against `'govHospital'`/`'regular'`) and `size` (string, used only as an equality check against `'xs'`/`'sm'`)
- All rendered values (`palette.bg`, `palette.fg`, `palette.short`, `palette.label`) are HARDCODED string literals selected by the equality check — no attacker-controlled value reaches the DOM
- JSX text interpolation auto-escapes; `style` receives a static object with hex color literals; `className` is a string literal
- No sink (no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, no eval, no URL, no shell, no path, no SQL, no logging)
- The call-site change `<ReservedPoolBadge pool={seatLabelPool(seatId)} size="xs" />` only passes a literal `"xs"`, which still only selects a hardcoded palette branch

No source→sink path exists. No findings.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 22:49:10Z)**_

No security findings. The diff is a UI-only addition of an extra "xs" size variant to a presentational badge in a Vercel mockup file. All rendered values are hardcoded constants selected by string equality on the `pool` prop, JSX escapes text, and no dangerous sinks (innerHTML, eval, exec, fetch, fs, SQL, logging) are touched.
